tasks.title: Agent Reading Pack v1.1 Front-Door Hardening
tasks.description: Implement Agent Reading Pack v1.1 front-door hardening with task-specific required reading, canonical-only insufficiency boundaries, sidecar usage rules, answer-compliance prompts and prohibited claims. The pack remains a derived navigation index; canonical_md remains the only content truth.

tasks.title: Agent-Pack-Usage Smoke
tasks.description: Added a deterministic producer-level smoke test for the Agent Reading Pack v1.1 task-to-artifact usage matrix, canonical-only insufficiency rules and sidecar authority boundaries. No schema, CLI command or producer behavior was added.

tasks.title: Retrieval Review Structural Goldset v1
tasks.description: Completed the structural review retrieval goldset subtask with 20 versioned queries, all 15 blueprint categories, loader-compatible metadata, and static structure and target validation. The reproducible metric baseline, expected-target hit reporting, and miss diagnostics required by the full blueprint slice remain open in TASK-AGENT-FRONTDOOR-004.

tasks.title: Retrieval Review Metric Baseline + Miss Diagnostics
tasks.description: Added a review-goldset adapter (review_eval.py) that measures the 20-query review goldset against the existing eval_core metrics (recall@10, MRR, zero_hit_ratio, per-category recall/MRR) and reports per-expected-target hits (target, kind, found, rank) plus a miss-taxonomy summary reconciled with the existing eval_diagnostics taxonomy. The baseline doc is no longer structural-only. Retrieval, ranking, indexing, and routing runtime are unchanged; no embeddings, reranking, schemas, sidecars, or CLI were introduced. The baseline is diagnostic and does not establish review completeness or retrieval quality.

tasks.title: Review Retrieval Goldset Self-Reference Hygiene
tasks.description: Add exact, safe, default-empty repository-path exclusions before SQL ordering and LIMIT, and thread them only through query execution and evaluation. When an explicit repo_root is supplied, the review baseline excludes the repository-local goldset with reason goldset_self_reference and records that this is a changed measurement condition, not a ranking improvement. No ranking, router, CLI, bundle, embedding or Guard Relation production is introduced. Status done: merged via PR #800 (d1726ea3) and post-merge reconciled on main.
missing_evidence: No ranking quality improvement is claimed or established.
Review-intent routing and path/symbol boosting remain later Retrieval v2 slices.

tasks.title: rLens Source Acquisition Control-Plane Hardening
tasks.description: Harden the rLens source-acquisition control plane: a single source-mode validator (validate_source_mode_request) is enforced at /api/jobs (HTTP 422 before job hash, reuse and any git/network) and identically on the CLI, repoLens headless (exit 2) and the WebUI (blocked submit with a visible error). local_ff+plan_only is rejected (never silently coerced to local_current); remote_ref and an explicit non-default remote_ref_policy are rejected on non-remote_snapshot modes (no job-hash drift). The source_acquisition_report now has a real JSON-Schema contract validated against written reports in tests, and tar extraction uses a manual, link-rejecting writer (no tarfile.extract).

tasks.title: Planning Registration Guard
tasks.description: Introduce a foundational guard that checks whether active planning artifacts (blueprints, roadmap docs, planning specs, status reports) are registered in task-control or roadmap. The originally deferred ratchet/baseline/frontmatter-contract layer was delivered by TASK-OPS-CTL-005, which promoted the guard from report-only to a baseline-backed ratchet.
missing_evidence: Superseded by TASK-OPS-CTL-005 (ratchet/baseline/frontmatter contract now implemented).

tasks.title: Planning Drift Ratchet Control Plane
tasks.description: Promote the planning-registration guard from report-only to a baseline-backed ratchet control plane. Known legacy drift is tolerated via a deterministic baseline (stable, line-number-independent finding ids); new unregistered planning artifacts and invalid/expired frontmatter exemptions block CI with exit 1. Resolved baseline entries surface as resolved/stale (non-blocking). A finding is identified by sha256(code\0normalized_path\0kind)[:16] so ids do not move when documents are reordered. The scanner gained --format human|json, --baseline, --ratchet and --update-baseline; JSON is emitted on stdout only, human text on stderr. A draft-07 report contract (lenskit.planning_registration_report.v1) validates the machine-readable report. CI runs the ratchet, uploads the JSON report as an artifact, writes a GitHub step summary (current/baseline/known/new/resolved/invalid counts), and blocks on new findings, invalid exceptions, or control/config errors (exit 2). Invalid exemptions are never written into the baseline.
missing_evidence: Auto-remediation (baseline pruning of resolved entries, GitHub PR comments) remains a deliberate follow-up.
Scanner scope stays heuristic (explicit globs + doc_type filter); no full content-classification of planning artifacts.

tasks.title: Real Dump Surface Self-Check Gate
tasks.description: After final bundle emission the standard dump path runs a read-only surface self-check: it persists post_emit_health and bundle_surface_validation sidecars, records machine-readable links (post_emit_health_path, bundle_surface_validation_path, bundle_surface_validation_status) and stamps generator.runtime provenance. A single-repo dump shipping docs/doc-freshness-registry.yml can no longer silently lack the claim_evidence_map surface while output_health still reads pass: the map is present XOR a machine-readable absence reason, and an active surface defect aborts emission. output_health stays a pre-emit signal; this gate is additive and is never a truth/forensic verdict. The work was originally implemented in fe6723d, then silently reverted by a graft in PR #736; this task restores it (verified against current main) and adds standard-dump-hook integration coverage.
missing_evidence: forensic_strict CI-Promotion bleibt ein separater PR (Nicht-Ziel dieses Auftrags).

tasks.title: rlens post-merge restart smoke
tasks.description: After a `git pull`/merge in `repos/lenskit`, the systemd --user `rlens.service` MUST be restarted (`systemctl --user restart rlens`) before any new dump is treated as evidence of the current code state. Python loads modules at process start; a long-running service keeps emitting from its frozen module snapshot, which previously produced dumps without `generator.runtime`, `links.post_emit_health_path`, `links.bundle_surface_validation_path`, `links.bundle_surface_validation_status`, and `claim_evidence_map_json`. This task adds a runbook proof, a non-automating comment in the systemd unit, and a smoke script that asserts the surface fields against the latest `*_merge.bundle.manifest.json` and that the agent reading pack carries no legacy `claim_evidence_map is not yet produced` placeholder. No core bundle/surface code is changed.
missing_evidence: Keine Core-Änderung; der Slice bleibt bewusst operativ. Eine echte Service-Automation (z. B. ExecStartPre=git pull, Post-Merge-Hook mit `systemctl --user restart rlens`) wird explizit nicht eingeführt (Begründung im Proof).

tasks.title: Forensic Strict CI Canary
tasks.description: Adds a controlled, path-scoped GitHub Actions canary that runs governance forensic-preflight against a small, hermetic, registry-bearing fixture bundle and persists a machine-readable JSON report as a build artifact. This promotes the existing F2c calibration into CI visibility without making forensic_strict a global blocking policy and without changing core bundle emission, claim-map production or the surface validator. The canary builds the fixture through the standard merge pipeline (scan_repo + write_reports_v2, max_bytes=100_000), verifies the post_emit_health and bundle_surface_validation sidecars emitted by write_reports_v2 and asserts a strict status=pass. A pre-existing diagnostic bug was fixed as part of this slice: commit 39f27873 moved claim_evidence_map derivation from the lenskit package registry to the scanned repo's own docs/doc-freshness-registry.yml (correct surface parity), which left the registry-less calibration fixture unable to produce claim_evidence_map_json; both proof fixtures now ship a minimal schema-valid registry. PASS means the formal forensic_strict prerequisites are present; it is explicitly not a truth verdict and not a license to answer without citations.
missing_evidence: Full blocking forensic_strict promotion to a required check remains a follow-up after several stable canary runs across bundle profiles.

tasks.title: Output Noise Hygiene A2
tasks.description: Exclude local scratch, CI-canary, and tool-cache noise from standard Lenskit dumps while preserving meaningful repository dotdirs. The scanner now skips the .tmp path segment along with existing cache/build noise, surfaces bounded excluded-noise diagnostics through output_health, and lets post_emit_health inherit the validated noise-hygiene signal.
missing_evidence: Full .gitignore semantics remain a non-goal; dump policy uses explicit scanner skip segments.
Noise ratchet or CI-blocking for newly discovered noise patterns remains a follow-up PR.

tasks.title: Real-Dump Noise Hygiene Runtime/Surface Proof
tasks.description: Verifies that the TASK-NOISE-001 scanner/output-hygiene policy is active in service-style dumps, not only in fixture tests. This slice diagnoses the real service path, confirms the current runner uses scan_repo() and write_reports_v2(), hardens the post-merge surface smoke, and completes the missing operator-host proof: after systemctl --user restart rlens, a fresh real dump at /home/alex/repos/merges/lenskit-max-260604-0705_merge.bundle.manifest.json passed scripts/rlens-post-merge-surface-smoke.sh with noise_surface_check=pass, structured_path_absent=.tmp/forensic-preflight-ci-canary, excluded_noise_count=450, output_health_noise_available=true, and post_emit_health_noise_available=true. The earlier contradictory dump is therefore explained as runtime drift from a long-running service, not as a current core scanner/writer codepath bug.
missing_evidence: Full .gitignore semantics remain out of scope.
Noise ratchet or CI-blocking for new noise patterns remains a follow-up.

tasks.title: Pythonista/iPad validation capability fixture
tasks.description: Adds a deterministic Pythonista/iPad-like degraded-runtime fixture for missing jsonschema, proving that validation degradation remains machine-readable and does not imply corrupted artifacts or forensic readiness.

tasks.title: Check-Shape Consistency Audit
tasks.description: Inventarisiert und stabilisiert die aktuellen Check-Shapes von output_health (Dict/Mapping), post_emit_health (Liste von Check-Objekten) und bundle_surface_validation (Liste von Check-Objekten); dokumentiert die Consumer-Pfade, bewertet die Formdifferenz als bewusst/rollenbedingt (Summary vs. geordnetes Check-Log) statt als Drift und entscheidet ohne Producer-/Schema-Migration, dass spätere Normalisierung nur additiv erfolgen darf. Ergänzt einen fokussierten Regressionstest, der die aktuell akzeptierten Shapes festschreibt. Keine Producer-, Schema-/Contract-, CLI- oder Dependency-Änderung.
missing_evidence: Keine Producer-/Schema-/Contract-/CLI-Migration und keine checks-Shape-Vereinheitlichung in diesem Slice (bewusstes Nicht-Ziel).

Folge-Optionen bleiben additiv: read-only Normalisierungs-Helper bzw. gemeinsame CheckView; eine echte Schema-Migration nur mit explizitem Kompatibilitätsplan, der den isinstance(dict)-Silent-Degradation-Pfad aller fünf output_health-Dict-Consumer adressiert.

tasks.title: Bundle/Test Fixture Builder
tasks.description: Extraktion einer test-only Fixture-Infrastruktur für wiederkehrende Bundle-/Health-/Surface-Testdaten. `test_validation_check_shapes.py` importiert keine privaten Helper mehr direkt aus anderen Testmodulen. Ursprungstests sind schonend angebunden und bewahren exakt ihre etablierten Semantiken.

tasks.title: Additive Read-only CheckView
tasks.description: Additiver read-only Adapter für die drei checks-Surfaces (output_health Mapping, post_emit_health Liste, bundle_surface_validation Liste). CheckView ist eine frozen dataclass mit name, status, detail, validation, value, raw, container_shape. Scalars (z. B. chunk_count, manifest_present) werden nicht zu Status-Checks umgedeutet (status=None). Duplikat-Namen in Listen werden deterministisch behandelt (iter_check_views liefert alle in Reihenfolge, checks_by_name gibt letzten Eintrag zurück). Keine Producer-, Schema-, Contract- oder CLI-Migration; kein __init__.py-Export erforderlich.
missing_evidence: Consumer-Migration: bestehende OH/PEH/BSV-Consumer greifen weiterhin direkt auf die Raw-Shapes zu — CheckView ist Consumer-seitig opt-in.
Structured ValidationError Objects bleiben separater Slice.
Loader-Konsolidierung (safe_load_yaml/json) bleibt separater Slice.

tasks.title: Facet Model v1 (Contract/Core/Tests)
tasks.description: Add a deterministic, additive Facet Model v1 as a contract/core/test slice. Introduces three controlled v1 facets (contract, test, retrieval), each derived from a single controlled path/suffix source_rule with derivation_type direct only. Facets are additive navigation view-axes (cardinality 0..n): exactly one Primary Lens remains, LENS_IDS and infer_lens() are unchanged, and possible_facets stays an empty placeholder. The lens-family negative-semantics baseline is enforced at report and item level. The taxonomy is deliberately small; artifact_surface, diagnostic, claim_boundary, security, uncertainty and the guard half of test_guard are deferred with reasons. Non-goals: CLI, bundle/manifest emission, Lens Cards, Relations/States, retrieval integration, and any review/security/impact verdicts. The defined contract/core/test slice is merged and post-merge verified; TASK-LENS-FACET-001 is complete.
missing_evidence: Taxonomy is intentionally partial (3 of the candidate facets); the rest are deferred by decision.
No CLI, bundle emission, possible_facets population, Lens Cards, Relations or retrieval integration.

tasks.title: Lens Cards v1 (Contract/Core/Validation/Tests)
tasks.description: Add deterministic Lens Cards v1 as a contract/core/validation/test slice. Defines one Card per accepted repo path, with path as the v1 identity within a single explicit repository context (does not prove file existence or tracked-file membership). Each Card projects the existing public Primary Lens explanation (including matched_rule) plus Facet Model v1 assignments from infer_facets(), with exactly one navigation-only repo_path reference to the same path and fixed Lens-family negative semantics. The semantic validator checks schema shape and producer coherence by recomputing the expected Card from path. Non-goals: review, safety or impact semantics; CLI, bundle, manifest or consumer integration; Relations, States, Task Contexts, PR Delta Cards, possible_facets population and retrieval ranking. Status done: Merge and post-merge reconciliation successful.
missing_evidence: CLI, bundle, manifest and consumer integration are outside this task.
Relations, States and PR Delta Cards are outside this task.
Actual agent usefulness or retrieval usefulness is not established.

tasks.title: Planning Ratchet Auto-Remediation
tasks.description: Adds --prune-baseline auto-remediation to the planning registration ratchet: default dry-run, only --write removes exactly currently-resolved baseline entries. New findings are never added. Invalid exceptions, control errors, unreadable baselines and a mutating write that would remove the last baseline entry block fail-closed (exit 2); an already empty baseline is an unchanged no-op (exit 0). No PR comment write permissions; contents: read is preserved.

tasks.title: Portable validation diagnostics proof
tasks.description: Documents the portable validation diagnostics; no production code or schema change.

tasks.title: Explicit rLens Service Restart Control
tasks.description: Separates browser hard-refresh from the optional local service restart. POST /api/admin/restart is default-off (RLENS_ENABLE_SERVICE_RESTART=1), loopback+auth gated, validates RLENS_SERVICE_UNIT fail-closed, blocks while jobs are running and schedules the restart decoupled via systemd-run --user --on-active=1s. No git pull, no auto-restart, no remote admin.

tasks.title: rLens Pre-Pull vor Merge (bounded repo-sync mutation)
tasks.description: Two-phase fast-forward-only pre-pull (plan/apply) across all surfaces; effective_pre_pull = pre_pull and not plan_only; opt-out via pre_pull flag (default on). Writes pre_pull_report.json on effective pre-pull success/hard-fail; no report on skipped pre-pull. Cloning missing repos, full omnipull integration and self-repo auto-restart remain explicit non-goals.

tasks.title: rLens Source Acquisition Remote Snapshot
tasks.description: Source modes local_current/local_ff/remote_snapshot. No git pull always: the no-upstream case is solved via remote_snapshot + default_branch (origin/HEAD, fallback origin/main) without mutating the local working tree, upstream or branch. Remote snapshot is a job-bound content projection (isolated bare cache + git archive), not a hub clone. Writes source_acquisition_report.json (schema lenskit.source_acquisition_report.v1) with provenance. Cloning missing repos remains a separate task; submodules/LFS are not expanded (report warnings).

tasks.title: Validation diagnostics schema alignment proof

tasks.title: Validation diagnostics regression gaps
